<div class="blog-header">

## [Curate People](https://nav.al/curate-people)

<div class="blog-subtitle">

On recruiting and culture

</div>

</div>

<div class="content">

You’re listening to the Naval Podcast. Today we’re going to be talking
about recruiting, hiring, team, and culture. There’s a famous quote from
[Vinod Khosla, “The team you build is the company you
build.”](https://x.com/vkhosla/status/1445973802282745857?lang=en) Or in
other words, they told you it was a technology game when it’s really a
recruiting game.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/curate-people" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="blog-header">

## [In the Arena](https://nav.al/in-the-arena)

<div class="blog-subtitle">

On Elon, iteration and agency

</div>

</div>

<div class="content">

Welcome back to the Naval Podcast. I’ve pulled out some tweets from
Naval’s Twitter from the last year, and we’re just going to go through
them. **Inspiration All the Way Down**  Here’s actually my first
question. You told me that you got an early copy of [the Elon
book](https://www.elonmuskbook.org/) from [Eric
Jorgenson](https://x.com/EricJorgenson).<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/in-the-arena" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Find the Simplest Thing That Works](https://nav.al/simplest)

</div>

<div class="content">

We’ve all seen the pictures of [the Raptor engine for the SpaceX
rockets](https://x.com/SpaceX/status/1896700492883079267), and if you
look at the various iterations, they go from easy-to-vary to
hard-to-vary. Because the most recent version just doesn’t have that
many parts that you can fool around with. The earlier versions have a
million different parts where you could change the thickness of it, the
width of it, the material, and so
on.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/simplest" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="audio-player-container">

<div class="controls">

</div>

</div>

6:18

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Good Products Are Hard to Vary](https://nav.al/good-products)

</div>

<div class="content">

I think reading [Deutsch](http://x.com/daviddeutschoxf/) across all the
different disciplines is very useful. Even when he talks about
[memes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Meme) and meme theory—that comes
from evolution, but crosses over straight into epistemology, conjecture,
and criticism. And it reaches far beyond his definition of wealth: the
set of physical transformations that you can
effect.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/good-products" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="audio-player-container">

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Most Books Should Be Skimmed, A Few Should Be Devoured](https://nav.al/get)

</div>

<div class="content">

For the state of the art on the philosophy of knowledge, which people
call [epistemology](https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/epistemology/),
you can basically skip everything and jump straight to [David
Deutsch](https://x.com/daviddeutschoxf). I think that’s right. If you
just want to know epistemology, read David Deutsch—full stop. That said,
for some people it helps to know the history, the counterarguments,
where he’s coming from.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/get" class="post-more-link">More <em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="audio-player-container">

<div class="controls">

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [The Best Authors Respect the Reader’s Time](https://nav.al/density)

</div>

<div class="content">

Unlike [Schopenhauer](https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/schopenhauer/),
you are an industrial philosopher. Like an industrial designer, your
philosophy is designed for the masses. People suggest you read the great
books—Aristotle and
[Wittgenstein](https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/wittgenstein/) and all
the supposedly great philosophers. I’ve read almost all that stuff, and
I’ve gotten very little value from
it.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/density" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="audio-player-container">

<div class="controls">

<a href="#" class="not-smooth play-pause-btn"></a>

</div>

3:03

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [It Is Impossible to Fool Mother Nature](https://nav.al/fool)

</div>

<div class="content">

You have to take responsibility for everything bad that happens to
you—and this is a mindset. Maybe it’s a little fake, but it’s very
self-serving. And in fact, if you can go the extra mile and just
attribute everything good that happens to you to luck, that might be
helpful too.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/fool" class="post-more-link">More <em></em></a>

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Blame Yourself for Everything, and Preserve Your Agency](https://nav.al/agency)

</div>

<div class="content">

Let’s talk about one more tweet which I liked when I first saw it, or I
might have retweeted it. I think people retweet things when they see
something that they haven’t figured out how to say yet, but they knew in
their head, but it’s just implicit—it hadn’t been made
explicit.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/agency" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="blog-header">

## [Pause, Reflect, See How Well it Did](https://nav.al/reflect)

</div>

<div class="content">

We talked about in the past how “Become the best in the world at what
you do. Keep redefining what you do until this is true.” And Akira made
a [song](https://youtu.be/EYB7zM_iXVY?si=CKUgADMVUk8sTnx3) out of it.
[Akira the Don](https://x.com/akirathedon?ref=mrdbourke.com), God bless
him. And I think that’s absolutely
true.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/reflect" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="audio-player-container">

<div class="controls">

</div>

<div class="blog-item-inner">

<div class="post-meta">

Aug 3 2025

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Hiring a Podcast Editor and Personal Chief of Staff](https://nav.al/hiring)

</div>

<div class="content">

We’re hiring an editor for the Naval Podcast, and Naval is also hiring a
personal chief of staff. If you’re not interested in either of these,
you can move on to the next episode. Let me give you some details on
both of them. First, the editor for the Naval Podcast, which as you
already know, is the most timeless and overproduced podcast in human
history.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/hiring" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="audio-player-container">
